title: Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary (or the Changthang Cold Desert Wildlife Sanctuary), a high-altitude wildlife sanctuary in the Ladakhi adjunct of the Changthang plateau in Leh distric
overview.description: Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary (or the Changthang Cold Desert Wildlife Sanctuary), a high-altitude wildlife sanctuary in the Ladakhi adjunct of the Changthang plateau in Leh district of Indian Union Territory of Ladakh, is one of the 3 largest wildlife sanctuaries in Ladakh, others being Karakoram Wildlife Sanctuary and Hemis National Park. It is 125 km east from Leh city, the capital of Ladakh.

topPlaces.name: Chakula
topPlaces.description: Chakula is a mountain peak located at 6,534 m (21,437 ft) above sea level in the far west of the Transhimalaya mountain range.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (116 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (332 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
